videos.title: Live at Shangri-La Dialogue; Iran Strike Injures Americans | Bloomberg This Weekend: May 28
desc_lines: Listen to Bloomberg This Weekend for unique conversations spanning news, politics, lifestyle and culture.
David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring you unique discussions and in-depth conversations with lawmakers, business leaders and cultural icons.
Bloomberg This Weekend gives you insight to make sense of the world.

videos.title: Trump Sends Mixed Signals on Iran, Dell Soars | Bloomberg Businessweek Daily 5/29/2026

chapters.title: Trump: Will Be Meeting Now to Make Final Determination on Iran
Quarter of Big Oil Tankers Trapped by Iran War Have Escaped
Dell Soars Most in 2 Years on Outlook

desc_lines: On this episode of Bloomberg Businessweek Daily, Tim Stenovec and Christina Ruffini discuss the latest on US-Iran negotiations with Chris Kennedy, Economic Statecraft Lead for Bloomberg Economics. They also discuss, Dell's strong earnings with Woo Jin Ho, Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Hardware and Networking Analyst. They also discuss the state of small business with Liz Wilke, JPMorgan Chase Chief Economist of Business Banking.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Businessweek Daily\r

videos.title: Trump Says He Will make 'Final Determination' on Iran | Balance of Power: Early Edition 5/29/2026

chapters.title: Stocks Hit Records High After US-Iran Deal Reports
Trump Says 'Final Determination' on Iran Is Coming
Oil Slides as Trades Bet on US-Iran Truce Renewal
Congress to Take Up War Powers Resolution Again After Recess
Bank of England's Bailey on Bloomberg

desc_lines: On the early edition of Balance of Power,  Bloomberg Washington Correspondents Joe Mathieu and Kailey Leinz discuss President Trump's signal that he will make decision whether to extend the fragile ceasefire with Iran for another 60 days. On today's show, former US Congresswoman from California Jane Harman, Harvard Kennedy School Ash Center Visiting Democracy Fellow Jeanne Sheehan Zaino, President and CEO of Republican Main Street Partnership Sarah Chamberlain, and Hermeus president Zach Shore. In addition, Governor of the Bank of England Andrew Bailey in conversation with Bloomberg's Stephanie Flanders from the Reykjavík Economic Conference.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Balance of Power: Early Edition\r

videos.title: Who's Worth More - Anthropic or OpenAI?
desc_lines: Anthropic PBC raised $65 billion in a funding round that valued the artificial intelligence company at $965 billion including the new investment, eclipsing rival OpenAI’s value for the first time.

The funding, announced Thursday, was led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks and Sequoia Capital. Each of the lead investors put in more than $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter. Sequoia declined to comment. The other three firms did not respond to a request for comment.
For more, we speak with Mandeep Singh, Global Head of Tech Research for Bloomberg Intelligence.\r

videos.title: What's Driving Markets This Morning? Dell is surging, Gap is falling
desc_lines: On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:
- Dell (DELL) shares are soaring after the company gave an outlook for annual sales that far surpassed analysts’ estimates, fueled by demand for servers that power artificial intelligence work.
- Gap (GAP) shares plunged after the company lowered its sales outlook due to struggles with its product mix.

videos.title: Stocks Rise as Trump Says He’ll Make Call on Iran
desc_lines: Hopes that a ceasefire deal could pave the way for an end to the Iran conflict drove stocks toward a historic streak of weekly gains, with the market also buoyed by the artificial-intelligence trade.
Brian Belski, CEO and CIO at Humilis Investment Strategies, discusses the S\u0026P 500 bull case and his outlook for markets amid the AI boom.\r

videos.title: Gap CEO Remains Bullish Despite Lowering Sales Outlook
desc_lines: Gap Inc. CEO Richard Dickson says the retailer delivered progress in the quarter, even after lowering its sales outlook. He says three out of their four brands are growing and they're raising the earnings outlook. Dickson speaks to Bloomberg's Lisa Abramowicz.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Rocket Belonging to Jeff Bezos Explodes on Launchpad in Florida
desc_lines: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded while undergoing a test on a launchpad in Florida on Thursday evening. The explosion is the latest blow to its reputation as a reliable alternative to SpaceX’s Falcon 9. (Source: @JConcilus via Associated Press)\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Stocks Hold at Record Highs on US-Iran Deal Optimism | Bloomberg Brief 5/29/2026

chapters.title: Wall Street Week: How Poland Became Europe’s Growth Standout

desc_lines: Stocks hold steady at all-time highs as traders wait to see whether the US and Iran can finalize a peace deal. SpaceX is said to cut its IPO valuation to at least $1.8 trillion while Anthropic's newest funding round puts its valuation at $965 billion eclipsing rival OpenAI. Cole Smead of Smead Capital Management discusses the 'mania' in tech stocks.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Brief\r

US, Iran Agree on Further Truce Pending Sing Off, Sources Say

Bessent: Trump Accounts Will Launch on July 4

desc_lines: On this episode of Bloomberg Businessweek Daily, Alexis Christoforous and Matt Miller speak with Jordan Fabian on the latest from Washington DC ahead of Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's press briefing. videos.title: US, Iran Truce Hopes Lift Stocks | Open Interest 5/28/2026

chapters.title: Snowflake Jumps on Sales Outlook, Amazon Deal
Retail Earnings Point to Consumer Resilience
Saira Malik, Nuveen | Stocks and Bonds Pare Losses After Inflation Data
High Interest | US, Iran Trade Truce , Tilman, Ferrari

desc_lines: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Stocks climb on reports the US and Iran may extend their truce, pending President Trump’s backing. Software splits as Salesforce disappoints, while Snowflake surges on AI demand. Plus, Goldman’s Ben Snider joins Bloomberg Open Interest on his higher S\u0026P target, Sridhar Ramaswamy, the CEO of Snowflake gives his outlook, Miriam Wheeler calls AI a "generational opportunity” and Kayak’ CEO Peer Bueller weighs in on the travel rally after news of the potential ceasefire.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Open Interest\r

videos.title: US Strikes Iran Targets; Snowflake Jumps on Results | Bloomberg Brief 5/28/2026
desc_lines: US equity futures fall and oil climbs after the US struck Iranian military targets for the second time this week. Snowflake surges after a stronger-than-expected annual outlook and signing a $6 billion deal with Amazon. Salesforce issues a tepid outlook. Michael Sneyd of BNP Paribas discusses Kevin Warsh's challenges as Fed Chair ahead of US PCE numbers.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Brief\r

videos.title: Micron Joins Trillion-Dollar Club | Open Interest 5/27/2026

chapters.title: AI Boom Fuels Memory Chip Stocks
Goldman Raises S\u0026P 500 Year-End Target to 8,000
Iranian Tv Says it Obtained Unofficial US-Iran Mou Draft
High Interest | Robinhood, Swiss Investor, Dick's Sporting Goods
Dick’s Sporting Goods Cuts Profit Forecast

desc_lines: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." AI powers markets to fresh highs, with Goldman calling for even more gains. Micron joins the trillion-dollar club as the chip frenzy accelerates. Nasdaq President Nelson Griggs joins Open Interest on the battle for blockbuster listings and the SpaceX IPO. Plus, Northern Trust’ Asset Management President Michael Hunstad explains why he’s staying risk-on in the AI boom.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Open Interest\r

videos.title: Micron Blows Past $1 Trillion on AI Demand
desc_lines: Micron has experienced a significant market surge, surpassing a $1 trillion valuation driven by robust demand in the artificial intelligence sector. Despite this, the company remains undervalued, trading at under 10 times forward earnings. Bloomberg Intelligence's Jake Silverman has more on "Bloomberg Open Interest."\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Ed Yardeni Says Dip-Buying Opportunities in This Market Are Wishful Thinking
desc_lines: Edward Yardeni, president at Yardeni Research, says the basic thrust of the market is higher as he touts “FEMO” or the “fabulous earnings momentum” he sees driving a melt up in stocks.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Goldman Strategists Cite Earnings in Hiking S&P 500 Target to 8,000
desc_lines: Strategists at Goldman Sachs increased their year-end target for the US benchmark to 8,000 points, ditching a previous forecast of 7,600. They see earnings growth powered by the AI boom driving further gains in stocks. Michael Msika reports on Bloomberg Television.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Hormuz Would Reopen One Month After Deal, Iran TV Says
desc_lines: An unofficial draft of a peace deal says the Strait of Hormuz would return to normal within a month, according to Iranian state television. It also says the the US naval blockade would end. Oil prices fell on the news. It's unclear what the US has agreed to. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall reports.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Memory Chip Frenzy Sends SK Hynix Into $1 Trillion Club
desc_lines: The breakneck surge in memory-chip stocks is intensifying, sending the market capitalizations of SK Hynix and Micron Technology above $1 trillion for the first time, as investors bet the AI boom will lead to a sustained revaluation of the industry. Bloomberg's David Savage breaks down the trends.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Stocks Extend Highs; Paxton Defeats Cornyn in Texas Senate Runoff | Bloomberg Brief 5/27/2026
desc_lines: AI optimism drives US stocks to another record high.  Market capitalization of SK Hynix and Micron hit $1 trillion. Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton defeated incumbent John Cornyn winning the state's GOP Senate runoff after securing an endorsement from President Trump.  Ella Gude of BNY Investments Newton discusses inflation risks.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Brief\r

videos.title: Taiwan Suspects Nvidia AI Chip Shipment Smuggled to China
desc_lines: Taiwan prosecutors suspect three individuals successfully smuggled at least one shipment of Nvidia AI chips to China after first exporting them to Japan, people familiar with the matter said. The trio was detained for allegedly falsifying documents related to exports of Super Micro Computer servers containing advanced Nvidia chips, which the US has barred from sale to China without a license. Bloomberg Intelligence's Matthew Bloxham breaks down the situation.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

desc_lines: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Markets are higher as hopes for a peace deal between the US and Iran overshadowed military strikes in the Persian Gulf. Eli Lilly makes a nearly $4 billion vaccine push and reports promising cholesterol drug results. Plus, SpaceX’s IPO roadshow is sending space stocks soaring, and Ferrari’s first $640,000 EV draws major backlash from fans.\r

00:00:00 - Open Interest\r

videos.title: Lilly Gets Back Into the Vaccine Business With New Deals
desc_lines: Eli Lilly \u0026 Co. is buying three clinical-stage vaccine developers for as much as $3.8 billion as it looks to become a leader in infectious diseases. The drugmaker has been using cash from its obesity drugs to expand into other disease areas. Bloomberg's Gerry Smith reports.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Oil Rebounds as US Launches New Strikes on Iran
desc_lines: Oil rebounded as fresh US military strikes in Iran clouded the outlook for an interim deal between Tehran and Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Stephen Stapczynski reports.\r
More on Bloomberg Television and Markets\r

videos.title: Oil Rebounds After US and Iran Clash Near Hormuz | Bloomberg Brief 5/26/2026
desc_lines: Brent crude rebounds after US and Israeli jets struck Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after President Trump suggested peace talks were progressing. Katrina Dudley of Franklin Templeton discusses inflation risks and consumer pressure.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Brief\r

videos.title: US Stocks Eye Longest Stretch of Weekly Gains Since 2023 | Bloomberg Brief 5/22/2026
desc_lines: The Pentagon weighs scrapping a conditional loan offer to rare-earths refiner ReElement Technologies amid an agreement that was meant to help break China's dominance on critical minerals. Shares of Spanish retail group Puig Brands drag as merger talks with Estee Lauder falls through. Priya Misra of JPMorgan Asset Management discussed the economic implications of a prolonged conflict in the Middle East. \r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Brief\r

videos.title: Nvidia Shares Muted After Fluctuation; Iran Assesses Trump's Proposal | Bloomberg Brief 5/21/2026
desc_lines: US equity futures fall as Iran says it is assessing President Trump's latest peace proposal. Shares of Nvidia pauses post-earnings fluctuation after results faced investor skepticism. Frank Lee of HSBC analyzes Nvidia's results and forecast. Abigail Watt of UBS discusses the Fed minutes and the labor market.\r
Chapters:\r
00:00:00 - Bloomberg Brief\r

videos.title: Stocks Rebound Ahead of Nvidia Earnings; GOP Scores Win in Kentucky | Bloomberg Brief 5/20/2026
desc_lines: US equity futures rebound as investors await earnings from Nvidia Client. Ed Gallrein, President Trump's hand-picked candidate for a Kentucky congressional seat, defeats longtime GOP House member Thomas Massie in one of the most expensive primaries on record. Amy Wu Silverman of RBC Capital Markets discusses this earnings season. \r
Chapters:\r
